name: blueprinteventbus-integration
description: Implement event-driven communication using BlueprintEventBus for cross-module coordination. Use this skill when modules need to communicate without tight coupling, broadcasting domain events (task.created, member.added), subscribing to events with proper lifecycle management, and implementing event-driven workflows. Ensures events follow naming conventions ([module].[action]), include Blueprint context, and use takeUntilDestroyed() for automatic cleanup.
capabilities: Publish domain events with Blueprint context
Subscribe to events with lifecycle management using takeUntilDestroyed()
Implement audit logging via event subscriptions
Create workflow orchestration with event chains
author: 7Spade
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing TypeScript code examples for Angular event-driven patterns. All 99 static findings are false positives triggered by pattern-matching errors: (1) Hash strings in JSON metadata (content_hash, tree_hash) flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms, (2) GitHub source_url flagged as hardcoded URL vulnerability, (3) TypeScript template literal backticks flagged as shell backtick execution, (4) Domain event names like task.assigned flagged as C2/reconnaissance keywords, and (5) Angular lifecycle hooks flagged as system reconnaissance. This is pure documentation with no executable code, network calls, file system access, or security-sensitive patterns.
valueStatement: Modules in large applications often become tightly coupled, making updates difficult and error-prone. This skill provides patterns and code examples for implementing BlueprintEventBus to enable loose coupling through events.
limitations: Service implementation code must be written by developer
No runtime event monitoring or debugging provided
Does not generate event types automatically

useCases.title: Add event-driven patterns
useCases.description: Implement BlueprintEventBus in Angular services for decoupled module communication.

useCases.title: Standardize event naming
useCases.description: Establish naming conventions like [module].[action] across teams for consistent event structures.

useCases.title: Connect UI and backend
useCases.description: Use events to trigger notifications and update UI when backend operations complete.

promptTemplates.title: Basic event setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to inject BlueprintEventBus in an Angular service and publish a task.created event with blueprintId and actor fields.

promptTemplates.title: Subscribe with filtering
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Angular component code that subscribes to task events but only reacts to events from a specific Blueprint using input() and takeUntilDestroyed().

promptTemplates.title: Audit logging system
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an AuditLogService that subscribes to ALL events globally and logs them to a repository with full event metadata.

promptTemplates.title: Workflow automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a TaskWorkflowService that listens for task.completed events and automatically updates dependent tasks and notifies stakeholders.

bestPractices: Always include blueprintId in events to enable Blueprint-specific filtering
Use takeUntilDestroyed() to prevent memory leaks in components
Publish events AFTER successful operations to maintain consistency
antiPatterns: Do not use EventBus for simple parent-child component communication; use @Output instead
Avoid circular event chains that cause infinite loops
Do not use events for synchronous request-response patterns; use services directly

faq.question: What versions of Angular does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports Angular 16+ with the inject() function and takeUntilDestroyed() API for lifecycle management.

faq.question: What are the naming limits for event types?
faq.answer: Event types follow the [module].[action] pattern. Use lowercase letters, dots, and action verbs like created, updated, deleted.

faq.question: Can I use this with non-Angular frameworks?
faq.answer: The patterns apply to any RxJS-based system, but code examples require Angular-specific features like inject() and DestroyRef.

faq.question: Is my event data stored or transmitted anywhere?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides patterns. Your implementation controls where event data is stored or sent.

faq.question: Why are my event subscriptions not working?
faq.answer: Common issues include: missing takeUntilDestroyed() causing cleanup, subscribing after events publish, or missing blueprintId filtering.

faq.question: How is this different from Angular Signals?
faq.answer: EventBus handles cross-module communication. Signals are for reactive state within components or services. Use both together.
